gigapan.description:
SantaCon Vienna occured.  It was pretty awesome.

Here the Santas are gathered in the courtyard of the Museumsquartier having Punsch.

Things were peaceful until the Santa's started sliding on heir bellies down the Curling Pitch, competing for distance.  They were asked to stop, and then the Curling Attendant cleaned up the ice with his shovel.

Then there were carols, including the classic "I wish I could see you naked," to the tune of 'We wish you a Merry Christmas."   After a fairly patient interval things decayed, a little bit.  I started to set up for a GigaPan from the platform of the curling pitch and an official told me not to.

"This area is not for singing.  Who is the leader of this group."
"Well, " I started, "There is no leader."

He had different ideas and found a leader and encouraged a peaceful Santa Exodus.

Some people, including families with small children, seemed delighted by it all, and others were horrified at some of the antics and some of the lyrics.  There was a lot of excitement and pictures taken and happiness and then there was definitely a bit of unhappiness mixed with the shock, joy, and amazement.



From Wikipedia http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SantaCon
"SantaCon is a mass gathering of people dressed in their various interpretations of Santa Claus costumes and performing publicly on streets and in bars in cities around the world. The focus is on spontaneity, creativity, and the improvisational nature of human interaction while having a good time and spreading cheer and goodwill to all they come across."
